annotations.label: Real projective line

anchor.snippet: a real projective line is a projective line over the real numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the projectivization of any vector space over a division ring; the real projective line is the special case ℙ ℝ (Fin 2 → ℝ), not separately named.

annotations.label: Formal definition of P(R)

anchor.snippet: a real projective line P ( R ) is defined as the set of all one-dimensional linear subspaces of a two-dimensional vector space over the reals

annotations.note: equivSubmodule gives the bijection between ℙ K V and the one-dimensional submodules (finrank = 1), generalizing the real two-dimensional case.

annotations.label: Projective transformations

anchor.snippet: The automorphisms of a real projective line are called projective transformations

annotations.note: Injective (semi)linear maps induce maps on ℙ via Projectivization.map, but the automorphism group of the projective line is not bundled or named.

annotations.label: Automorphisms form PGL(2,R)

anchor.snippet: They form the projective linear group PGL(2, R )

annotations.note: PGL(2,R) is defined, but the theorem that it is exactly the automorphism group of the projective line is not formalized.

annotations.label: Homeomorphic to circles

anchor.snippet: real projective lines are homeomorphic to circles

annotations.note: No homeomorphism between the real projective line and a circle is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalence relation on V

anchor.snippet: Define on V ∖ 0 the binary relation v ~ w to hold when there exists a nonzero real number t such that v = t w

annotations.note: projectivizationSetoid is the setoid on nonzero vectors given by the Kˣ scalar-orbit relation, exactly the v ~ tw relation.

annotations.label: P(V) as set of equivalence classes

anchor.snippet: The real projective line P ( V ) is the set of all equivalence classes

annotations.note: Projectivization is defined as the quotient of nonzero vectors by projectivizationSetoid, i.e. the set of equivalence classes.

annotations.label: Projective coordinates notation

anchor.snippet: The equivalence class of the pair ( x , y ) is traditionally denoted

annotations.note: Projectivization.mk constructs the equivalence class of a nonzero vector, but the homogeneous-coordinate bracket notation [x : y] is not provided.

annotations.label: Projective line as quotient of circle

anchor.snippet: the projective line may be considered as the quotient space of the circle by the equivalence relation such that

annotations.note: The realization of the projective line as the quotient of the circle by antipodal identification is not formalized.

annotations.label: Projective line is a manifold

anchor.snippet: The projective line is a manifold

annotations.note: There is no ChartedSpace or manifold instance on the projectivization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Transition map and analytic manifold

anchor.snippet: The transition map between these two charts is the multiplicative inverse

annotations.note: No chart atlas or transition map giving an analytic manifold structure on the projective line is present.

annotations.label: Projectively extended real line embedding

anchor.snippet: It defines an embedding of the real line into the projective line, whose complement of the image is the point

annotations.note: OnePoint.equivProjectivization gives a set-theoretic equiv OnePoint K ≃ ℙ K (Fin 2 → K) with coe t ↦ [t:1] and ∞ ↦ [1:0], but only as an equivalence, not as a topological embedding.

annotations.label: Association with antipodal points

anchor.snippet: Points of the real projective line can be associated with pairs of antipodal points on a circle

annotations.note: The correspondence between projective points and antipodal point-pairs on a circle is not formalized.

annotations.label: Complete projective range

anchor.snippet: The real projective line is a complete projective range that is found in the real projective plane and in the complex projective line

annotations.note: The synthetic-geometry notion of a complete projective range is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cyclic order on the projective line

anchor.snippet: The real projective line has a cyclic order that extends the usual order of the real numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has the CircularOrder typeclass but no cyclic-order instance on the real projective line extending the order of ℝ.

annotations.label: Right action of GL2(R) on row vectors

anchor.section: The projective linear group and its action
anchor.snippet: Matrix-vector multiplication defines a right action of GL 2 ( R ) on the space R 2 of row vectors

annotations.note: Mathlib gives a right module action of square matrices on vectors via vecMul (and a left one via mulVec); GL₂(R) on R² is a special case.

annotations.label: Induced action on P1(R)

anchor.section: The projective linear group and its action
anchor.snippet: there is an induced action of GL 2 ( R ) on P 1 ( R )

annotations.note: Any group acting linearly on V (including the general linear group) gets an induced MulAction on ℙ K V.

annotations.label: Projective linear group

anchor.section: The projective linear group and its action
anchor.snippet: The projective linear group is defined to be the quotient group PGL 2 ( R ) = GL 2 ( R )/ R ×

annotations.note: ProjGenLinGroup is GL n R quotiented by its center, which equals the scalar matrices Rˣ for a field, matching PGL₂(R) = GL₂(R)/R×.

annotations.label: Faithful action of PGL2(R)

anchor.section: The projective linear group and its action
anchor.snippet: there is an induced faithful action of PGL 2 ( R ) on P 1 ( R )

annotations.note: Machinery to build a PGL action from a scalar-trivial GL action exists, but a faithful PGL action on the projective line is not instantiated or proven faithful.

annotations.label: Action by linear fractional transformations

anchor.snippet: one obtains a corresponding action of PGL 2 ( R ) on R ∪ ∞ , which is by linear fractional transformations

annotations.note: The GL(2,K) action on OnePoint K acts on finite points by the Möbius/linear-fractional formula (g00·k+g01)/(g10·k+g11).

annotations.label: Sharply 3-transitive action

anchor.snippet: there exists a unique element of PGL 2 ( R ) mapping the first triple to the second; that is, the action is sharply 3-transitive

annotations.note: The action on ℙ K V is proven 2-pretransitive, but sharp 3-transitivity of PGL on the projective line is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cayley transform

anchor.snippet: the linear fractional transformation mapping (0, 1, ∞) to (−1, 0, 1) is the Cayley transform

annotations.note: The Cayley transform as a specific linear fractional transformation is not formalized (only Cayley graphs/Cayley's theorem exist).

annotations.label: Stabilizer of the point at infinity

anchor.snippet: The stabilizer in PGL 2 ( R ) of the point ∞ is the affine group of the real line

annotations.note: The stabilizer of ∞ is characterized by g 1 0 = 0 (upper-triangular matrices), but its identification with the affine group is not stated.
